meta.vector: v3.5 - WorldSession persistence SOAK (long-horizon + multi-cycle suspend/resume + seal)
meta.note: Generated by the real TS suspend/resume + catchUpEpochs. BUNDLE FORMAT v3: every embedded bundle carries a structural ChainSeal (seal field) AND a signed identity binding (binding field); resume() on EVERY surface (TS/Python/Rust) verifies both fail-closed - the binding first. Five cases: S1 pins 120-epoch catch-up composability (four 30-epoch chunks == single-shot, with 30/60/90 checkpoints); S2 pins the zero-catch-up resume boundary and mid-chain suspend (snapshotEventIndex 2) snapshot-position independence; S3 pins three suspend->resume->append->re-suspend cycles on ONE chain == one 21-epoch resume; S4 pins the sealed 21-record chain verifying across the whole gap (tests add the truncation/tamper negative space - bare verifyRecords without a seal cannot see a cut tail, which is why the bundle now CARRIES the seal structurally); S5 pins void-at-scale (resolve 100 of 500, void 400) and a deterministic SECOND resume across the void boundary. Rust (golden_session_soak.rs) and Python (test_world_session.py) consume these cases; the format matches what golden_epoch.rs / golden_session.rs parse.

cases.label: S1 long-horizon-catchup-120: chunked == single-shot over 120 epochs
S2 boundary-suspend-resume: zero-catch-up boundary + mid-chain suspend
S3 multi-suspend-cycles-3x7: suspend -> resume -> append -> re-suspend, one chain
S5 void-at-scale: 400 epochs voided, then resume across the void
